checks.a_analysis: The conversion result contains only text found in the source document. No fabricated sections, headings, or body text were identified. The metadata title is an expected AI addition for accessibility.
The only URL present in the document is 'http://it.nc.gov/gicc', which matches the source exactly. No other contact information like phone numbers or emails were present or fabricated.
All primary agenda items (1 through 11) are present in the output. The meeting dates and website information at the bottom of the page are also included.

The main agenda table in the source has 11 data rows plus a header. The JSON output represents these 11 rows within its table structure (reading_order 6). While sub-items of Item 8 are extracted as a list (reading_order 7), the main table structure itself is complete.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: In Agenda Item 8 (Committee Reports), the conversion failed to capture several significant pieces of information: the names of the individuals presenting for each committee (Ryan Draughn, Kathryn Clifton, John Farley, Doug Newcomb, Dan Madding, Stan Duncan) and the specific voting items for the Statewide Mapping Advisory Committee and Local Government sections. This represents a loss of more than 50% of the content for this specific agenda item.
checks.b_evidence_source:
• Statewide Mapping Advisory Committee Ryan Draughn
 o Vote: Standard for Parcel Data Content (Summary)
• Local Government Kathryn Clifton
 o Vote: LGC Bylaws Revised
• State Government GIS Users John Farley
• Federal Interagency Doug Newcomb
• Technical Advisory Dan Madding
• Management & Operations Stan Duncan

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Besides the significant omissions identified in C5, the text that was converted is accurate to the source. Dates, times, and names in other rows are correct.

checks.a_analysis: The output does not contain any duplicated content. Each section and agenda item from the source PDF appears exactly once in the JSON structure.
The heading hierarchy follows a logical structure. The main title and subtitle use H1, and 'AGENDA' uses H2. No heading levels are skipped.

The table structure for Item 8 ('Committee Reports') is corrupted. In the source document, Item 8 contains a list of committees with corresponding presenters in the 'GICC MEMBER or GUEST' column. In the output, the table row for Item 8 has an empty third cell, and the presenter names (e.g., Ryan Draughn, Kathryn Clifton) associated with the committees are missing entirely from the structure. Furthermore, the table continues to Items 9, 10, and 11, while the content that should be inside Item 8's row is moved outside.
checks.b_evidence_source: Item 8, Committee Reports row showing names like 'Ryan Draughn' and 'Kathryn Clifton' in the right-hand column.
checks.b_evidence_output: The table row for item 8 (reading_order 6) contains an empty cell: '"text": ""' in the GICC MEMBER or GUEST column.

checks.a_analysis: The reading order is significantly disrupted regarding Item 8. In the source, the committee list and presenters follow immediately after '8. Committee Reports' and before '9. Statewide Orthoimagery Program'. In the output, the entire table (Items 1-11) is processed first (reading_order 6), followed by the list of committees (reading_order 7). This means a user would read Items 9, 10, and 11 before receiving the detailed breakdown of the committees in Item 8.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source document presents the committee list between agenda item 8 and agenda item 9.
checks.b_evidence_output: The JSON output places the committee list (reading_order 7) after the entire table (reading_order 6) which includes agenda items 9, 10, and 11.

checks.a_analysis: The conversion of the list under 'Committee Reports' is incorrect. It fails to capture the nested 'Vote' items (e.g., 'Vote: Standard for Parcel Data Content' and 'Vote: LGC Bylaws Revised') which are essential sub-agenda items. Additionally, it loses the structural association between committee names and the presenters listed next to them in the source.
checks.b_evidence_source: • Statewide Mapping Advisory Committee Ryan Draughn\n    o Vote: Standard for Parcel Data Content (Summary)
checks.b_evidence_output: The list item only contains: 'Statewide Mapping Advisory Committee'. The nested vote bullet and the presenter name are missing.

checks.a_analysis: The output fails to identify and preserve several hyperlinks present in the source document. Specifically, the 'Minutes' link in agenda item 3 is rendered as plain text. The vote-related links are missing entirely due to the structural list failures noted in S5.
checks.b_evidence_source: 3. Approval of Minutes of May 11th Meeting (where 'Minutes' is a blue, underlined hyperlink)
checks.b_evidence_output: {"text": "3. Approval of Minutes of May 11th Meeting", "bold": false, "italic": false, "underline": false ...}

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output shows a significant visual reorganization of the agenda content. In the source PDF, Agenda Item 8 ('Committee Reports') is rendered as a complex item within the main table, containing a nested list of committees in the 'AGENDA ITEM' column and corresponding presenters in the 'GICC MEMBER or GUEST' column. In the rendered HTML, this structure is broken: the 'GICC MEMBER or GUEST' column for row 8 is left blank, and the list of committees is moved out of the table into a separate bulleted list below it. This layout change detaches the committee reports from their time allocation and presenter information, which is also partially missing in the output.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF on page 1 shows item 8 within the table containing a nested list of committees (e.g., Statewide Mapping Advisory Committee) and their presenters (e.g., Ryan Draughn) aligned across columns.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows Item 8 in the table with an empty third column, and a bulleted list of committee names starting after the table ends. The presenters' names are missing from this list.

checks.a_analysis: The main agenda table is visually misrendered in the HTML. Specifically, row 8 fails to display the sub-structure and associated presenter data present in the source PDF. This results in a large empty cell in the 'GICC MEMBER or GUEST' column and a loss of the logical grouping between the 'Committee Reports' heading and the specific committees listed. The sub-content of the table has been 'leaked' out of the table container into the main body text below, breaking the table's visual and structural integrity.
checks.b_evidence_source: In the source PDF, page 1 shows a 3-column table where row 8 contains a list of 6 committees and their presenters integrated into the row structure.
checks.b_evidence_output: In the HTML screenshot, the table row for item 8 is missing the presenters' names in the 'GICC MEMBER or GUEST' column, and the committee list is rendered as standard bullet points outside the table.

checks.a_analysis: The meaningful image from the source (the NCGICC logo) is present in the HTML output. Although its position has shifted from the top-center to the bottom of the page, it is rendered correctly and not broken.

There is no evidence of duplicated body content. While titles and headings appear in both the navigation 'Contents' sidebar and the main document body, this is an expected accessibility feature and does not constitute a defect.
